symbols: lily in a vase — purity of the Virgin and the fragrance of the Incarnation
open book before Mary — her prayerful study of Scripture, often understood as Isaiah's prophecy of the virgin
angel's white robe — the heavenly messenger bearing divine tidings
red bed canopy and furnishings — the bridal chamber, the womb that will receive the Word
hands folded in prayer — Mary's receptive obedience to God's will
description: An interior domestic scene in a Flemish-style bedroom. At left, an angel in a pale blue-white robe with large white wings enters, one hand raised in greeting. At right, the Virgin Mary kneels on the floor beside a wooden bench, wearing a deep blue robe, her hands folded in prayer and her gaze lowered toward an open book. Between them at center-bottom, a glass vase holds white lilies. Behind the angel, a red-draped bed with a canopy fills the left portion of the room. A window at right admits natural light. The floor has a checkered tile pattern.
theological_description: The Annunciation is the hinge of salvation history, the moment when the angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will conceive and bear a son (LUK.1.26-38). Van der Weyden sets this cosmic event within an ordinary Flemish bedroom, emphasizing the incarnational theology that the infinite God enters the finite world through the most humble means. Mary's prayer posture and her open book suggest she was meditating on Scripture — tradition holds she was reading Isaiah 7:14, the very prophecy she would now fulfill. The lily, symbol of virginal purity, affirms the doctrine of the virginal conception. The red bed canopy functions as a symbol of the bridal chamber where the Holy Spirit will overshadow Mary (LUK.1.35), making her the new Ark of the Covenant bearing the divine presence within her womb. Mary's response, 'Let it be to me according to your word' (LUK.1.38), becomes the definitive human act of faith — the new Eve's 'yes' undoing the old Eve's disobedience.

scripture_refs.reason: The angel Gabriel is sent to Mary to announce the conception of Jesus
The Holy Spirit will come upon you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you
Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son — the prophecy Mary fulfills
Behold, I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word
The protoevangelium — the woman's seed who will crush the serpent's head, beginning at this conception
